_note: SAMPLE / DEMO DATA — for prototype only. Pre-built example gameplans used to showcase output and as a fallback. The live app generates a similar plan from a user's intake answers. Not official VA guidance.

examples.headline: Land softly: benefits enrolled, a job pipeline, and nothing left behind.
priorities: Lock in VA health care and a disability claim before you separate
Build a civilian job pipeline now, not after your last day
Decide education vs. work — and use the right benefit for it

plan30.text: Finish TAP and safeguard your DD-214 + medical records
File a VA disability claim (ask about Benefits Delivery at Discharge)
Draft a civilian resume translating your MOS into plain language

plan60.text: Apply for VA health care and pick a facility near home
Register with a Texas American Job Center + DOL VETS rep
Request your GI Bill Certificate of Eligibility if school is likely

plan90.text: Have 3–5 job leads or an enrollment plan in motion
Meet a free accredited VSO to review your claim
Check Texas benefits (Hazlewood, property tax) — verify with the state

longTerm: Use the VA home loan when you're ready to buy
Consider VR&E if a service-connected condition affects your work
Revisit your gameplan every 90 days as life changes
whyItMatters: Filing before separation can shorten the wait for a decision.
Enrolling in VA health care early protects access to care and mental health support.
A job pipeline started now prevents an income gap later.

examples.headline: Secure the next chapter: pay, protection, healthcare, and purpose.
priorities: Confirm retirement pay and Survivor Benefit Plan elections
Coordinate VA health care with Medicare/TRICARE for Life
Get documents and beneficiaries fully organized

plan30.text: Review retirement pay statement and SBP election
Locate and back up all key documents in one secure folder

plan60.text: Coordinate VA care with Medicare/TRICARE for Life

plan90.text: Check Florida veteran property-tax exemptions — verify with the state
Plan community and purpose for the next chapter

longTerm: Review Aid & Attendance eligibility if care needs increase
Keep survivor documents current for your family
whyItMatters: SBP decisions are time-sensitive and hard to reverse.
Coordinating coverage avoids gaps and surprise costs.
Organized documents protect your family if something happens to you.
